rulings.Case Title: VITALIY NEVIDOMY ET AL VS. MARAT DINER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2017 line 3. PLAINTIFFs VITALIY NEVIDOMY and VOLHA SUSHKO's Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED. The subpoena is overbroad and seeks information that is not even claimed to be relevant and necessary to defendant. Defendant chose not to narrow the scope of subpoena during meet and confer process to (1) inquire regarding Plaintiff's presence at work on the dates of alleged harassment incidents and (2) inquire regarding Plaintiff's work schedule/work hours during the time immediately preceding and following the termination of tenancy. Request for sanctions is granted as requested. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VITALIY NEVIDOMY ET AL VS. MARAT DINER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Granting Leave To Permit Discovery Within 30 Days Of Trial
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2017 line 3. DEFENDANTs MARAT DINER, KIRA DINER, and IGOR TYRYSHKIN's Motion For An Order Granting Leave To Permit Discovery Within 30 Days Of Trial DENIED. Moving party failed to meet the requirements of C.C.P. 2024.050. Request for sanctions is denied. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD E THOMAS VS. CHET BENTLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2017 line 4. Defendant'S Demurrer To First Amended Complaint sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts in support of causes of action for breach of contract and common counts showing that these causes of action are not barred by the statute of limitations. Plaintiff shall be mindful of the "sham pleading" doctrine. Plaintiff's opposition and reliance on relation back doctrine is not well-taken as Plaintiff did not avail himself of CCP 474 and did not substitute William Bentley in place of a Doe. See original complaint and FAC - both pleadings name Does 1-10, i.e. FAC does not name "Chet Bentley, William Bentley as Doe 1, and Does 2-10", it names "Chet Bentley, William Bentley and Does 1-10". = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHUNG KING LAW VS. LYZANN POBRE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2017 line 7. DEFENDANTs LYZANN POBRE, GUI LIANG, and YU WEI's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Request for Judicial Notice of the Trust is denied. Issues of fact (including authority of one of the co-trustees to act alone) cannot be properly addressed on a demurrer. = (501/REQ)
